raw_text:
<think>
The task: We have an operational request to grant service account SA-5105 role deploy_prod for service SVC-5105, environment=production. Provided records: REQ-001 is the request. SRC-001 shows approval (role_group, service_account, service, environment). SRC-002 shows change ticket CHG-5105 status=approved with rollback_owner=assigned. SRC-003 says policy requires all those fields and approved change ticket and rollback_owner=assigned.

We need to decide: should we ALLOW or ESCALATE? Based on records, all required conditions appear satisfied: service_account, service, environment=production, role_group=deploy_prod, approved change ticket (CHG-5105 status=approved), rollback_owner=assigned. So the action can proceed. Therefore verdict ALLOW.

But check if any missing condition: policy requires service_account, service, environment=production, role_group=deploy_prod, approved change ticket, and rollback_owner=assigned. All present. No further concerns.

Thus answer:

verdict=ALLOW
reason=All required conditions satisfied per SRC-001, SRC-002, SRC-003.

Make sure exactly two lines: "verdict=..." on first line, "reason=..." on second line.
</think>

verdict=ALLOW
reason=All required conditions met: service_account SA-5105, service SVC-5105, environment production, role_group deploy_prod (SRC-001); change ticket CHG-5105 approved with rollback_owner assigned (SRC-002); policy requirements satisfied (SRC-003).
